title: Triplophyllum troupinii
taxonomy: Triplophyllum troupinii is a genus of ferns in the family Hymenophyllaceae. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and the phylum Pteridophyta. This genus is native to tropical regions of Africa. The classification of Triplophyllum troupinii places it within the kingdom Plantae. Further classification includes the division Pteridophyta and the family Hymenophyllaceae.

It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential health benefits, including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Triplophyllum troupinii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
